scenarios.rationale: The operating system (Windows Server 2019) is already on a current version per the provided lifecycle rules.

scenarios.scenario_name: Switch to standard Linux Operating System
scenarios.rationale: The current stack (Windows Server 2019, Java 17, Microsoft IIS 10.0) shows platform coupling that blocks a simple move to standard Linux.
The application is already deployed on AWS and uses a portable stack, so ARM-based hosting is a credible optimization option.
The recorded server technology (Microsoft IIS 10.0) is not a target application server replacement candidate in the provided rules.

scenarios.scenario_name: Application Migration to Cloud Infrastructure (Lift & Shift)
scenarios.rationale: The application is already deployed in AWS.
The application is already marked as containerized.
The solution type (3rd party software) suggests packaged software, which limits custom refactoring opportunities.
The database (Oracle 19c) is already current according to the provided lifecycle rules.

scenarios.scenario_name: Switch DB Engine to open-source database solution
scenarios.rationale: The database (Oracle 19c) is proprietary and the application is packaged software, so switching engines may not be vendor-supported.
Known components are current, but some technologies could not be dated from the available application data.
